signals.title: GBP/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + NFP Surprise
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3360 — June Nonfarm Payrolls miss estimates, driving the USD to a two-week low as Fed rate hike fears subside. GBP is the week's G10 leader (+0.714%) while USD is the laggard (-0.757%), creating a 1.471% spread. Despite a neutral 0bp OIS carry spread (Fed 3.68% vs BoE 3.73%), GBP outperformance is supported by a positive CESI (+24) vs USD's potential peak data regime. Bull steepener in the US curve (+76.1bp) confirms recessionary pricing following the jobs miss.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Intervention Risk + Options Skew
signals.text: USD/JPY at 160.95 — Price holds above the critical 160.00 threshold previously associated with BoJ intervention. Options market is aggressively hedging downside with a 25d RR of -1.09 (moderate put skew) and an elevated VRP of +3.2% (IV 7.2% vs HV30 4.0%). JPY COT LF net short -115,033 remains crowded; a sustained break above 160.00 increases the probability of direct BoJ action despite the +275bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5706 — COT LF net short -23,314 (specs fading hawkishness) contradicts the RBNZ hike bias priced for the 8 July meeting. With NZD real rates at -0.83% (accommodative) and a positive CESI (+6), a hawkish delivery or signal from the RBNZ triggers significant short-squeeze risk. Retail sentiment is 80% long, aligning with the institutional squeeze potential if the 0.5800 level is reclaimed.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Retail Divergence + Correlation Break
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4184 — Retail sentiment is 92% short (extreme), diverging from the COT LF net short of -87,096 (specs also short). This rare alignment of retail and institutional shorts suggests extreme CAD bearishness. However, the USD/CAD vs WTI correlation has broken to -0.871 (z = -1.94σ), indicating the pair is ignoring oil weakness ($67.8) to trade purely on USD macro. Watch for retail capitulation if USD/CAD tests 1.4300.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Volatility Premium + Carry
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.6932 — Options market pricing extreme protection with IV at 23.5% vs HV30 6.8% (VRP +16.7%). Despite the high vol premium, the +60bp AUD carry advantage (RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75%) and COT LF net long +29,035 support the pair on dips. 25d RR at -0.89 indicates the market is paying for downside protection despite the bullish positioning and carry profile.
